allowed: Edit sdk-core, sdk-claude, sdk-codex, lite-lint, focused tests, aligned docs and OKRA records
Add a shared session contract only after accepted discovery
Run local and real-provider integration checks
Commit and push only after all active walls hold
approval_gates: Human-only frame, threshold, metric, action-envelope, release, MCP, and tool-exposure changes
Human approval before irreversible external actions
forbidden: Change the public model consumer seam
Implicitly expand filesystem or execution authority
Kill tmux contributor sessions before the release gate
Force push, destructive reset, or discard user work
action_envelope.rollback: Keep changes in narrow reviewable commits and preserve the pre-run main revision

anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-2 defines and funds a deterministic graph-edge checker
Inspect child exit and transport disposal after close and cancel

anti_goals.activation_condition: Roots, permissions, auth, environment, or process spawning changes
anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-3 defines provider-specific denial probes

anti_goals.activation_condition: Public provider handles or model selection changes
anti_goals.read_method: Run provider type tests and compare exported model seam
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Existing type tests and public export assertions

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any session contract, package, release, or documentation change
anti_goals.read_method: Assert no MCP/tool collection implementation or publication is included
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Changed-path and public export audit plus npm registry read

anti_goals.read_method: Run gates for every touched surface and distinguish pre-existing failures
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Focused tests, root gates, and baseline comparison

anti_goals.read_method: Verify store and reject weakened, hidden, stale, unsupported, or self-certified claims
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Append-only run store and independent deterministic evidence

anti_goals.read_method: Build lint tool and scan every touched source, test, example, and doc path
Run strengthened dependency lint and dependency-resolution conformance tests

anti_goals.activation_condition: Config, engine, transport, auth, or provider wiring changes
anti_goals.read_method: Run no-implicit-binding lint and prove missing bindings fail during dependency resolution
Reject module mocks, global patches outside adapter tests, internal reaches, and non-scope test seams
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Test imports, lint diagnostics, and public-API conformance harness

anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-L1 determines a deterministic namespace-alignment read

anti_goals.activation_condition: Namespace, facade, flow, or exported object changes
anti_goals.read_method: Every effectful child call must resolve to a declared dependency edge

anti_goals.activation_condition: Definition handles or wiring types change
anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-L1 narrows mechanically provable ceremony rules
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Lint diagnostics and deterministic API-shape assertions

anti_goals.read_method: Require a syntactically provable invariant for every diagnostic
Run valid fixtures and count unexpected diagnostics
Compare known unsupported syntax classes with documented limitations
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Rule documentation and invalid fixture matrix

anti_goals.activation_condition: Provider code or lint configuration changes
anti_goals.read_method: Changed-path audit rejects provider-specific suppression additions
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Changed lint configuration and source suppressions

anti_goals.read_method: Compare touched paths with ratified provider, lint, test, docs, and OKRA scope
Require at least one passing and one failing fixture per new rule shape
Run identical lint inputs twice and compare normalized diagnostics
Important evidence must resolve by hash or governed source record

anti_goals.activation_condition: Run artifacts or product files are written
anti_goals.read_method: Important writes use the helper or carry a target plus content hash
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Move records, content hashes, and changed-path audit

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any DKR checkpoint, progress claim, or completion claim
anti_goals.read_method: Reject acceptance backed only by the orchestrator narrative
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Deterministic replay and independent review artifacts

anti_goals.read_method: Reject CKR without accepted DKR and PKR without accepted CKR
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Run tree and accepted checkpoint refs

anti_goals.max_age: current turn for committing moves; 10 minutes for live workers
anti_goals.missing_data_rule: Fail closed and open breaking or cannot
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Named checker, test, registry, Git, or append-only record per wall

objective.lag_rule: Immediate after complete conformance and smoke run
objective.read_method: Count providers with every readiness criterion and active wall held
objective.source_of_truth: Shared conformance results plus real Claude and Codex integration probes

objective.definition: Count Claude and Codex providers that pass start, prompt and normalized streaming, cancellation and cleanup, explicit roots and permissions, shared conformance, real integration smoke, and active authoring walls.

dkrs.decision_target: Choose the managed Claude session transport and lifecycle
dkrs.output: Executable DKR checkpoint with replayable probes and confidence

dkrs.decision_target: Define the smallest shared session contract without hiding graph edges
dkrs.output: Executable DKR checkpoint, contract proposal, lifecycle diagram, and conformance cases

dkrs.decision_target: Define explicit roots, permissions, environment, credentials, and denial behavior
dkrs.output: Executable DKR checkpoint and provider grant matrix

dkrs.decision_target: Classify authoring doctrine into reliable lint, compile/API assertions, or scope conformance tests
dkrs.output: Executable DKR checkpoint with valid, invalid, false-positive, and false-negative examples

orchestrator.terminal_condition: Continue until the objective metric reaches target, a human changes or stops the frame, or a blocking flag requires human resolution

payload.cause: Independent replay exposed environment-dependent permissionMode output; causal attribution was unsupported, so permission enforcement hands back to DKR-3

payload.decision_target: Choose the managed Claude session transport and lifecycle
payload.max_age: 7 days or any Claude CLI version change
questions_answered: Claude stream-json is a supported candidate session transport without MCP
Structured init, assistant, result, hook, usage, permission-denial, and session identity events are available
Roots, tool selection, permissions, session identity, persistence, and hook events have explicit CLI controls
questions_unanswered: Multi-turn stdin lifetime and interrupt acknowledgement
Direct execFile permission enforcement across inherited environments
payload.read_method: Replay the help read and one tools-disabled stream-json turn, then inspect init, assistant, result, tool count, and session IDs
payload.replay_command_or_checker: printf '%s\n' '{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Reply with exactly: PUMPED_SESSION_OK"}}' | claude -p --verbose --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json --tools '' --permission-mode dontAsk --no-session-persistence
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: AG-1 risk reduced: session IO can remain behind declared flows and a scope-owned resource
AG-3 remains open: permission enforcement is not accepted until direct execFile probes agree
AG-5 held: MCP and automatic tool collection are unnecessary for this session-control milestone
payload.source_of_truth: Installed Claude Code 2.1.207 help, two live stream-json probes, and independent Claude Opus 4.8 High replay

payload.dispatch_ref: DKR-3 direct process and authority probe

payload.cause: Initial checker expected SIGINT to remain the process exit signal; Claude handles it and exits code 0 after emitting a cancellation result
evidence_refs_or_hashes: command:node drafts/claude-cancel-probe.mjs exit 1 with result/error_during_execution and dead PID

payload.cause: Discovery worker wrote progress.jsonl directly instead of through okra-store worker-report; raw content was preserved and the log was rebuilt through the helper

payload.cause: The first namespace identity assertion omitted the existing codexAcpTurn import; the focused test exposed it before handoff
evidence_refs_or_hashes: command:pnpm --filter @pumped-fn/sdk-codex test -> ReferenceError codexAcpTurn is not defined

accepted_conclusions: AG-2 requires bounded graceful shutdown, SIGKILL escalation, and a second fail-closed bound
Claude and Codex need lifecycle hardening PKRs
Static graph evidence must include the provider boundary resource
Double close must terminate external resources exactly once
payload.rejection_reason: The frozen AG-3 metric counts undeclared roots and permission grants, not credentials. Credential declaration remains AG-10 work supported by DKR-3, not by this checkpoint.

orchestrator.terminal_condition: Continue until the objective metric reaches target, a human changes or stops the frame, or a blocking flag requires human resolution

payload.policy_residual: Codex grant remains explicit and coarse; changing or removing it requires a human policy decision
payload.reason: The challenger identified coarse but explicit caller policy, not an undeclared root or permission grant. Codex grant requires the caller to select grant, command and args are declared adapter configuration, and the installed codex-acp 1.1.2 binary does not parse process.argv. Removing explicit grant remains a human policy change outside this frame.

continuation_packet.next_action: Human opens a successor release frame before package publishing, Pages deployment, or tmux contributor cleanup
